result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The search results returned several papers focusing on uncertainty quantification and calibration in medical AI. Paper [4] stands out as the most relevant to the planned citation purpose because it explicitly addresses model degradation under dataset shifts, evaluates reliability using standard calibration metrics like Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and Brier score, and applies post-hoc calibration techniques (temperature scaling). It directly supports the argument that accuracy alone is insufficient for real-world deployment and highlights the pitfalls of model overconfidence. I will select this paper to cover concepts of miscalibration, established evaluation methodologies, and the research gap in uncertainty-aware deployment frameworks.
